data.title: HGreg is finalist in Dunamis competition in two categories - Company of the Year – Export and Technologies and Innovation.
data.description: A company in Montréal, HGreg, has been named a finalist in the Dunamis competition by the Laval Chamber of Commerce and Industry. They are nominated in two key categories: Company of the Year – Export and Technologies and Innovation. This recognition showcases the company's prowess in innovation and success in a dynamic industry. In the Export category, HGreg is commended for its creative approach in entering new markets, leading to substantial commercial growth. The Technologies and Innovation nomination emphasizes their commitment to advancing in these areas.

data.title: Michael Saylor's social media stirs Bitcoin debate, adding fuel to a controversial proposal among enthusiasts.
data.description: Michael Saylor's recent social media posts have intensified a debate within the Bitcoin community. This has led to increased discussions among Bitcoin enthusiasts.

data.title: Lisa Cook pleads for staying at Fed, deeming fraud claims weak. Ex-Fed chairs & Treasury secretaries support, citing risk to bank’s autonomy.
data.description: Previous Federal Reserve chairpersons and United States Treasury secretaries have expressed their support for the continued tenure of Ms. Cook. They emphasized the importance of preserving public trust in the central bank's autonomy and urged for her retention in the interim period.

data.title: ATOM price may reach $4.72 in 1 week, backed by oversold RSI & $4.05 Bollinger Band support.
data.description: Forecasts for ATOM's price show a potential increase to $4.72 in the next week. This prediction is underpinned by the oversold Relative Strength Index (RSI) levels and its close proximity to the Bollinger Band support at $4.05.

data.title: Concentrix Corp. (NASDAQ: CNXC) Q3 earnings miss, lowered guidance; EPS $2.78 vs. estimate $2.86. Stock falling.
data.description: Concentrix Corp (NASDAQ: CNXC) published its Q3 earnings after Thursday's market close, along with a reduced fiscal 2025 earnings forecast below projections. The key numbers from the quarter reveal earnings of $2.78 per share, falling short of the estimated $2.86. The company's stock, CNXC, is currently dropping. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Partnership between Journeys International and MEJDI Tours aims to enhance global understanding through travel adventures.
data.description: In a joint announcement made on September 25, 2025, Journeys International and MEJDI Tours revealed their partnership aimed at enhancing global understanding through cross-cultural travel experiences. The collaboration seeks to provide unforgettable adventures, emphasizing the belief in the power of travel to foster connections between cultures and people. The partnership is expected to combine the expertise of both companies to offer enriching travel opportunities to a wide audience.

data.title: ADCOLOR to honor T.D. Jakes, Dani Monroe, and Carmen Smith with Lifetime Achievement Awards in 2025.
data.description: In a recent announcement made on September 25, 2025, it was revealed that ADCOLOR, an esteemed organization focused on fostering community within the creative sectors, is set to bestow honors upon T.D. Jakes, Dani Monroe, and Carmen Smith. These distinguished individuals will be recognized with the prestigious ADCOLOR Lifetime Achievement Award for their notable contributions and accomplishments in their respective fields.

data.title: Sophia Toh releases new book "The G.A.M.E. of Life," focusing on leadership and success; also co-author of "Leadership Unscripted."
data.description: Atlanta resident Sophia Toh, a renowned executive coach and leadership educator, has officially introduced her latest book, "The G.A.M.E. of Life: Illuminated Leadership, Fulfilled Life." In addition to this publication, Toh has contributed as a co-author to "Leadership Unscripted," a collaborative project showcasing the perspectives of women in leadership roles. This announcement was made on September 25, 2025, marking a significant milestone in Toh's career as she shares her insights with a global audience.

data.title: Intel CEO engages TSMC and Apple for funding and partnerships, reaching out to acquire potential collaborations.
data.description: The CEO of Intel Corp (INTC) Lip-Bu Tan is actively seeking funding and partnerships to drive the company's turnaround. He has engaged with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd (TSM) and Apple Inc (AAPL) for potential collaborations. Discussions began before President Trump's involvement, but gained momentum after the US acquired a nearly 10% stake in Intel. The Trump administration, led by Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, is also reportedly involved in these efforts. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.
